title: rosa × dumetorum var. campestris
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. campestris, commonly known as the field briar or wild rose, is a variant of the Rosa dumetorum species, characterized by its thorny stems and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum var. campestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the production of rosehip oil and other skincare products.
